greeting: Morning, Haniel. Clear calendar, no meetings — the whole day is yours.
hype: AZ Team billing goes from backend to browser today. Four UI slices in the morning, three GS completions in the afternoon — by 6 PM you'll have a working billing dashboard and a client project moving forward. Let's go.
notice: t19 (PDF pipeline) is the last remaining Phase 2 backend task for AZ Team AI — knock it out first so every frontend task that follows lands on a complete foundation.

schedule.title: PDF pipeline (DomPDF invoice template + AZ letterhead, shared Money)
Owner notification panel for revision_runaway events
Ground-truth corpus: download + vision-read 208 material-list files

tasks.title: PDF pipeline (DomPDF invoice template + AZ letterhead, shared Money)
tasks.notes: Last open Phase 2 backend task. DomPDF invoice template + AZ letterhead, shared Money formatter.

prep.what: Build the invoice/estimate PDF renderer using DomPDF with AZ Team AI letterhead so invoices can be attached to emails and downloaded.
prep.understand: Phase 2 billing backend is complete except this one task. DomPDF is already a dep (used in CT Pros and Module). The `DocumentTotalsCalculator` from Phase 2e computes the money; share it here. A `Money` value-object or helper centralises cent-to-formatted-string conversions so templates never do math.
approach: Locate existing DomPDF usage in the app (search `DomPDF` / `Barryvdh`) and copy the bootstrapping pattern into an `InvoicePdfRenderer` service class.

Create a Blade template at `resources/views/pdfs/invoice.blade.php` — letterhead (logo, AZ address), invoice header (number, client, due date), line-items table with qty/unit-price/total, subtotal/tax/grand-total footer.
Wire a `GET /invoices/{invoice}/pdf` route → `InvoiceController@pdf` that calls `InvoicePdfRenderer::render($invoice)` and returns a streamed download response.
Add a smoke test: assert the response mime-type is `application/pdf` and content-length is non-zero.
resources: AZ Team AI logo asset path in `public/images/`

tasks.title: Dashboard AR cluster + cashflow calendar
tasks.notes: Dashboard hub design was completed in Phase 1 (t8 done). AR tiles, aging, outstanding-by-client, cashflow calendar.

prep.what: Build the main dashboard page — AR summary tiles, aging breakdown, outstanding-by-client list, and a cashflow calendar showing upcoming invoice due dates.
prep.understand: The backend KPI/reporting module (Phase 2e t44) already computes AR aging and revenue snapshots. You're building the Inertia/Vue shell over it. The Dashboard hub design (Phase 1 t8) was spec'd — tiles: total outstanding, current/30/60/90+ buckets, by-client table. The cashflow calendar uses the shadcn/ui Calendar primitive (installed in t6) with day annotations for due amounts.
approach: Add a `DashboardController@index` Inertia response that fetches AR aging data from the reporting module — query `Invoice` where `status != paid` grouped by aging bucket, plus upcoming installments by due date.
Build the AR cluster in `Dashboard/Index.vue`: 4 KPI tiles (total, current, 30d+, 60d+, 90d+) using the existing card primitives, plus a mini table of top outstanding clients.
Add the cashflow calendar below — iterate the shadcn Calendar component, mark each day that has an invoice or installment due with the day's total amount as a badge.
Wire the Inertia prop so the component is reactive, test with seeded data.
resources: Dashboard hub design spec from Phase 1 (t8 completion notes)

tasks.title: Projects + Versions (billing terms, rollover UI)
tasks.notes: Project + ProjectVersion models with recurrence engine are fully built. Build the list + detail pages.

prep.what: Build the Projects index and Project detail pages — the entry point for the billing system where Haniel manages client projects and their billing versions.
prep.understand: `Project` has a client FK and many `ProjectVersion`s. A version defines billing terms (recurrence schedule, amount, start date) and the `RecurrenceEngine` mints installments from it. When a client re-contracts, the `RolloverEngine` voids open invoices and carries the balance to a new version. The UI needs to surface this lifecycle clearly.
approach: Wire `ProjectController@index` / `@show` as Inertia responses, eager-loading client, current version, and a count of open invoices per project.
Build `Projects/Index.vue` — a sortable table: client name, project name, current version status, total outstanding balance, a 'New Project' button.

Build `Projects/Show.vue` — project header (client, status), version history accordion (past versions collapsed, current expanded showing billing terms + open installment count), a 'Roll Over' button that hits `POST /projects/{project}/rollover`.
Add a `CreateProject` dialog: client picker (typeahead over Clients), billing type (recurring/one-off), amount, start date, recurrence frequency.
resources: Existing Inertia controller patterns in the app (e.g. `ClientController`)

tasks.title: Invoices list + detail (send/void/PDF)
tasks.notes: Invoice model with full lifecycle done. Build list + detail with Send/Void/PDF actions.

prep.what: Build the Invoices list and Invoice detail page — the core billing UI where Haniel sees and acts on every invoice.
prep.understand: The `Invoice` model has a full state machine (draft → sent → paid → void). `QBInvoiceSync` pushes to Stripe (hosted payment link). The PDF pipeline from t19 (this morning) provides the download. Detail page needs a payment timeline showing any `Payment` records and partial payments.
approach: Wire `InvoiceController@index` returning all invoices paginated, with status badge, client name, amount due, due date, and aging indicator.
Build `Invoices/Index.vue` — table with status color-coding (draft=gray, sent=blue, overdue=red, paid=green), filter tabs by status.

Build `Invoices/Show.vue` — invoice header (number, client, due date, status), line items table with subtotal/tax/total, action rail: 'Send' (POST → QBInvoiceSync + email), 'Void', 'Download PDF' (GET /invoices/{id}/pdf from t19), and a payment timeline below.
Wire the action endpoints and add confirmation dialogs for Send/Void.

tasks.title: Owner notification panel for revision_runaway events
tasks.notes: D4 Phase D follow-up. Surface revision_runaway events to the owner's inbox/panel.

prep.what: Add a notification/alert panel that surfaces `revision_runaway` project events to Guilherme (the owner) so he knows when a project has cycled through too many design revisions.
prep.understand: `revision_runaway` is a D4 event flag on projects that have exceeded the revision threshold. The GS-ERP has a notification/inbox system already in place. This task wires that event into a panel visible to the owner role — Guilherme needs to see flagged projects at a glance without digging into each one.
approach: Find the existing `revision_runaway` flag dispatch (search for `revision_runaway` across events/listeners/models) and confirm what triggers it.
Create a `RevisionRunawayNotification` or reuse the existing notification channel — emit it to the `owner` role when the event fires.
Add an owner-only notification panel (or inbox card) on the dashboard: list of projects in `revision_runaway` with project name, client, revision count, and a link to the project.
Write one test: dispatch the event on a project → notification created → panel query returns the project.
resources: Search `revision_runaway` in the GS-ERP codebase for the event class and trigger
Existing notification/inbox patterns in the app

tasks.title: QB integration mapping documentation
tasks.notes: Document estimate fields, pricing, QB account classes for the bookkeeper handoff.

prep.what: Write a concise QB integration mapping doc so Guilherme's bookkeeper understands exactly how GS-ERP fields map to QuickBooks — estimate lines, income accounts, expense categories, payment reconciliation.
prep.understand: The QB sync (`QBEstimateSync`, `QBInvoiceSync`, `QBExpenseSync`) is live and working. The bookkeeper is taking over QB data entry and needs to know: which QB income account each estimate type lands in, how material categories map to expense accounts, and how the payment pull reconciles QB payments back into the CRM. This is documentation, not code.
approach: Read `QBEstimateSync::payload()` and `QBItemSync::resolveIncomeAccountRef()` to extract the income account resolution logic and document it as a table: GS line type → QB account.
Read the expense account map in Settings (QB expense account map) and document: GS overhead category → QB expense account.
Document the payment flow: customer pays QB hosted invoice → `QBPaymentSyncer::syncPayments()` polls → `PaymentRecorder` writes to CRM.
Write the doc as `docs/qb-field-mapping.md` — a table per entity type (Estimate, Invoice, Expense) with known edge cases (6240 dup-DisplayName recovery, stale ItemRef handling).
resources: QB Settings page (account mapping) in the running app

tasks.title: Ground-truth corpus: download + vision-read 208 material-list files
tasks.notes: Foundation for the material-list AI. Download 208 ClickUp attachments, vision-read each to extract Julio's BOM form fields.

prep.what: Build the ground-truth training corpus for the material-list AI: download all 208 historical material-list attachments from ClickUp, run each through vision inference to extract Julio's BOM form fields, and save as structured JSONL.
prep.understand: Phase 67 is the AI layer that auto-extracts material quantities from drawings into Julio's standard form. The ground-truth corpus is the prerequisite for everything (t457 specialist extractor, t458 eval). The 208 files live as attachments on ClickUp tasks in the material-list list. Vision-reading gives you input→output pairs for the training/eval split. Quality beats speed here — if a file is ambiguous, mark it as uncertain rather than guessing.
approach: Get the ClickUp API token from `.env` (CLICKUP_API_TOKEN) and identify the list/folder ID for material lists — check `docs/spike/` for any prior reference to the ClickUp list ID.

Write `scripts/download_corpus.py`: paginate `GET /list/{listId}/task?include_closed=true`, for each task fetch `GET /task/{taskId}/attachment`, download each attachment file (PDF/image) to `data/corpus/raw/`.

Write `scripts/vision_read_corpus.py`: for each downloaded file, call Claude vision (`claude-opus-4-8`, recommended for accuracy) with a prompt that extracts Julio's form fields — item name, quantity, unit, dimensions, material type. Save as `data/corpus/gt.jsonl` with `{file, fields}` per line.
Run on 10 files first to validate extraction quality before the full 208-file run.
resources: `docs/spike/` folder in GS-ERP repo for prior ClickUp spike scripts
Claude API docs — `claude-opus-4-8` with vision (base64 image or file URL in `content` array)
Julio's material-list form template (a completed example from any past project)
